strengths: Strong benchmark performance across diverse datasets
weaknesses: May struggle with strongly correlated features
Assumes outliers deviate in at least one marginal dimension
ECOD.best_for: General-purpose outlier detection when speed and interpretability are priorities
ECOD.avoid_when: Features are heavily correlated and outliers only manifest in joint distributions

complexity.time: O(n^2 * d) for fast, O(n^3 * d) for default

strengths: Not affected by distance concentration in high dimensions
weaknesses: Computationally expensive, especially in default mode
Sensitive to noise in low-dimensional data
Requires sufficient neighbors for angle variance estimation
ABOD.best_for: High-dimensional datasets where distance-based methods suffer from curse of dimensionality
ABOD.avoid_when: Dataset is very large or low-dimensional where distance-based methods work well

strengths: Models tail probabilities via empirical copulas
weaknesses: Assumes feature-wise independence for tail modeling
May miss complex multivariate interactions
COPOD.best_for: Large-scale datasets where speed and interpretability matter and features are roughly independent
COPOD.avoid_when: Outliers only appear in joint distributions with strong feature dependencies

strengths: Robust to outliers in the training data
weaknesses: Only works on univariate data
Cannot capture multivariate relationships
MAD.best_for: Univariate outlier detection with a robust central tendency measure
MAD.avoid_when: Data is multivariate or relationships between features are important

strengths: Based on affinity and binding probability concepts
Perplexity parameter controls local vs global sensitivity
weaknesses: Quadratic complexity limits scalability
Sensitive to perplexity parameter choice
SOS.best_for: Medium-sized datasets where probability-calibrated outlier scores are valuable
SOS.avoid_when: Dataset is very large (>10K samples) due to quadratic complexity

strengths: Based on uniformity criterion from quasi-Monte Carlo theory
weaknesses: Quadratic time complexity
Assumes outliers deviate from uniform space-filling
QMCD.best_for: Detecting anomalies as deviations from uniform space-filling in moderate-dimensional data
QMCD.avoid_when: Dataset is very large or high-dimensional

weaknesses: Quadratic prediction time
Sensitive to bandwidth and kernel selection
Suffers from curse of dimensionality
KDE.best_for: Low-to-moderate dimensional data where non-parametric density estimation is desired
KDE.avoid_when: Data is high-dimensional or dataset is very large

Sampling.full_name: Rapid Distance-Based Outlier Detection via Sampling

complexity.time: O(n * s * d) where s is subset size

weaknesses: Randomness from sampling can affect reproducibility
May miss local outliers with small subset sizes
Sampling.best_for: Large-scale datasets requiring fast distance-based outlier detection
Sampling.avoid_when: Precise and deterministic results are required or dataset is small enough for exact methods

complexity.time: O(n * k * d^2) per EM iteration

weaknesses: Sensitive to initialization and number of components
Assumes Gaussian component distributions
EM convergence not guaranteed to global optimum
GMM.best_for: Data with multi-modal distributions that can be approximated by Gaussian mixtures
GMM.avoid_when: Data does not follow mixture-of-Gaussians assumption or is very high-dimensional

strengths: Effective when anomalies lie in minor principal components
weaknesses: Assumes linear relationships
Sensitive to the number of components selected
Cannot capture nonlinear anomalies
PCA.best_for: Datasets with linear structure where outliers deviate from main variance directions
PCA.avoid_when: Data has strong nonlinear structure or outliers align with principal components

strengths: Captures nonlinear relationships via kernel trick
Extension of PCA to nonlinear manifolds
weaknesses: Quadratic memory and time complexity
Kernel and hyperparameter selection required
Slower than linear PCA
KPCA.best_for: Moderately sized datasets with nonlinear structure
KPCA.avoid_when: Dataset is very large due to quadratic kernel matrix or a linear model suffices

strengths: Resistant to masking effects from outliers
weaknesses: Assumes data follows a Gaussian distribution
May fail in high-dimensional settings (d > n)
Computationally intensive for large d
MCD.best_for: Multivariate Gaussian-like data requiring robust covariance estimation
MCD.avoid_when: Data is non-Gaussian, very high-dimensional, or strongly nonlinear

strengths: Based on classical influence measures from regression
Interpretable as influence on regression fit
Works well when a regression model is appropriate
weaknesses: Requires a target variable (semi-supervised framing)
Assumes linear regression model
Not suitable for purely unsupervised settings without a model
CD.best_for: Identifying influential observations in regression settings
CD.avoid_when: No natural target variable exists or data is purely unsupervised

strengths: Strong theoretical foundation from SVM literature
weaknesses: Cubic training time for large datasets
Sensitive to kernel and nu parameter choice
Does not scale well beyond ~10K samples
OCSVM.best_for: Medium-sized datasets where a flexible decision boundary is needed
OCSVM.avoid_when: Dataset is very large or real-time training is required

strengths: Multiple deviation measures available (AAD, IQR, MAD)
weaknesses: Results can vary due to random projections
Requires tuning of number of iterations and measure
May underperform with nonlinear anomaly patterns
LMDD.best_for: Multivariate data where anomalies are detectable through linear projections
LMDD.avoid_when: Anomalies require nonlinear feature combinations to detect

strengths: Adapts to varying densities across the dataset
weaknesses: Quadratic complexity limits scalability
Sensitive to n_neighbors parameter
Difficulty with uniformly dense regions
LOF.best_for: Datasets with clusters of varying densities where local anomalies are of interest
LOF.avoid_when: Dataset is very large (>50K) or data is uniformly distributed

strengths: Better than LOF for certain pattern structures
Handles low-density patterns in neighborhood chains
weaknesses: Quadratic time and space complexity
Slow for large datasets
Marginal improvement over LOF in many cases
COF.best_for: Datasets where outliers lie along sparse connectivity paths between clusters
COF.avoid_when: Dataset is large or simpler methods like LOF already perform well

strengths: Combines clustering with local outlier scoring
Effective when data has clear cluster structure
weaknesses: Depends on quality of underlying clustering
Sensitive to number of clusters parameter
May fail if data does not cluster well
CBLOF.best_for: Data with well-separated clusters where outliers deviate from cluster structure
CBLOF.avoid_when: Data has no meaningful cluster structure or clusters are heavily overlapping

strengths: Multi-granularity outlier detection via alpha parameter
Does not require explicit k-neighbor parameter
weaknesses: Quadratic complexity makes it slow
Difficult to scale to large datasets
Parameter alpha requires tuning
LOCI.best_for: Small to medium datasets where automatic threshold selection is valued
LOCI.avoid_when: Dataset is large or faster LOF-based methods are sufficient

weaknesses: Assumes feature independence
Cannot capture multivariate interactions
Sensitive to number of bins
HBOS.best_for: Large-scale datasets where speed is critical and features are roughly independent
HBOS.avoid_when: Outliers only manifest through feature interactions or correlations

HDBSCAN.full_name: Hierarchical Density-Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise
strengths: Automatically finds clusters of varying density
Identifies noise points as potential outliers
weaknesses: Performance depends on min_cluster_size choice
May be slow for very large datasets
Outlier scores derived from cluster membership
HDBSCAN.best_for: Datasets with variable-density clusters where noise points are outliers
HDBSCAN.avoid_when: Data does not have cluster structure or very high-dimensional

weaknesses: Quadratic complexity limits scalability
Sensitive to distance metric and k choice
Struggles with varying density clusters
KNN.best_for: General-purpose distance-based outlier detection on moderate-sized datasets
KNN.avoid_when: Dataset is very large or has highly variable local densities

strengths: Effective when anomalies hide in subspaces
Reference set approach captures local structure
weaknesses: Quadratic complexity
Requires tuning of ref_set and n_neighbors
May miss anomalies not aligned with axis-parallel subspaces
SOD.best_for: High-dimensional data where outliers deviate in axis-parallel subspaces
SOD.avoid_when: Data is low-dimensional or anomalies require oblique subspaces to detect

strengths: Considers rotations to detect outliers across projections
weaknesses: Designed primarily for 3D data, may not generalize well to very high dimensions
Limited theoretical analysis compared to classical methods
ROD.best_for: Low-dimensional data where rotation-invariant outlier detection is desired
ROD.avoid_when: Data is high-dimensional or a well-tuned alternative is available

complexity.time: O(n * t * log(n)) where t is n_estimators

strengths: Linear time complexity with efficient implementation
Does not require distance or density computation
weaknesses: May struggle with local anomalies in dense regions
Axis-aligned splits can miss anomalies in correlated features
IForest.best_for: General-purpose anomaly detection especially on large or high-dimensional datasets
IForest.avoid_when: Anomalies are local density deviations or features are strongly correlated

INNE.full_name: Isolation-based Anomaly Detection Using Nearest-Neighbor Ensembles

complexity.time: O(n * t * s) where t is n_estimators, s is sample size

strengths: Handles local anomalies better than Isolation Forest
weaknesses: Requires tuning of sample size and number of estimators
Less established than Isolation Forest
INNE.best_for: Datasets where local density variations matter and Isolation Forest underperforms
INNE.avoid_when: A simpler method like IForest already works well

strengths: Uses deep random representations for better isolation
Extends isolation forest to representation space
weaknesses: More computationally expensive than standard IForest
Requires tuning of representation parameters
DIF.best_for: Complex datasets where standard Isolation Forest misses anomalies due to axis-aligned splits
DIF.avoid_when: Standard Isolation Forest performs well or dataset is small and simple

weaknesses: Performance depends on choice of base detector
Slower than single detector due to ensemble overhead
May not improve over base if features are all relevant
FeatureBagging.best_for: High-dimensional data with potentially irrelevant features
FeatureBagging.avoid_when: All features are relevant or a single strong detector suffices

LSCP.full_name: Locally Selective Combination of Parallel Outlier Ensembles
strengths: Locally selects the best detector for each region
weaknesses: Requires a list of pre-instantiated base detectors
Slower due to training multiple detectors
Complex internal selection mechanism
LSCP.best_for: Scenarios where diverse base detectors are available and local performance varies
LSCP.avoid_when: Only one detector type is appropriate or computational budget is limited

weaknesses: Random projections may miss certain anomaly patterns
Histogram-based scoring can be coarse
Sensitive to number of bins and cuts
LODA.best_for: Streaming or online anomaly detection with limited computational resources
LODA.avoid_when: Batch setting with enough time for more powerful methods

strengths: Accelerates large-scale outlier detection via approximation
Supports parallel execution of base detectors
Modular framework for combining multiple detectors
weaknesses: Approximation may reduce accuracy
Overhead from the acceleration framework
Requires choosing and configuring base estimators
SUOD.best_for: Large-scale datasets where running multiple detectors is desired but time is limited
SUOD.avoid_when: Exact results from a single well-chosen detector are preferred

strengths: Combines unsupervised representations with supervised XGBoost
High accuracy when labels are available
weaknesses: Requires labeled data (semi-supervised)
Depends on XGBoost library
Training is more expensive than unsupervised methods
XGBOD.best_for: Semi-supervised settings where some labeled anomalies are available
XGBOD.avoid_when: No labeled data is available or a purely unsupervised approach is needed

complexity.time: O(n * d * h * epochs) where h is hidden size

strengths: Reconstruction error is an intuitive anomaly score
weaknesses: Requires tuning of architecture and hyperparameters
May overfit on small datasets
Training can be unstable
AutoEncoder.best_for: Datasets with complex nonlinear structure where reconstruction-based scoring is appropriate
AutoEncoder.avoid_when: Dataset is small, tabular and simple methods suffice, or training time is limited

weaknesses: More complex to train than standard autoencoder
KL divergence term may dominate loss
Requires careful balancing of loss components
VAE.best_for: Datasets where probabilistic reconstruction scoring and smooth latent spaces are beneficial
VAE.avoid_when: Simpler autoencoder or non-deep methods work well, or dataset is very small

strengths: GAN-based approach generates informative outlier samples
Novel adversarial training framework for OD
weaknesses: GAN training instability
Sensitive to hyperparameters and architecture
Slow training compared to non-deep methods
SO_GAAL.best_for: Exploratory anomaly detection with GAN-generated reference outliers
SO_GAAL.avoid_when: Stable and fast results are required, or dataset is small

complexity.time: O(k * n * d * h * epochs) where k is number of generators

strengths: Multiple generators provide diverse outlier references
Better coverage of outlier space than SO_GAAL
weaknesses: Even more complex training than SO_GAAL
Multiple generators increase computational cost
Difficult to tune
MO_GAAL.best_for: Complex datasets where diverse generated outlier references improve detection
MO_GAAL.avoid_when: Computational resources are limited or simpler GAN approaches suffice

strengths: Learns a compact hypersphere around normal data
Combines deep learning with SVDD objective
weaknesses: Sensitive to network architecture choices
Risk of hypersphere collapse
Requires careful initialization
DeepSVDD.best_for: One-class anomaly detection where a compact normal data description is desired
DeepSVDD.avoid_when: Normal data is multi-modal or simpler one-class methods are sufficient

AnoGAN.full_name: Anomaly Detection with Generative Adversarial Networks

complexity.time: O(n * d * h * epochs) + O(n * iterations) for inference

strengths: GAN learns the normal data distribution
Anomaly scoring via reconstruction in latent space
weaknesses: GAN training instability
Slow inference due to iterative latent optimization
Requires significant tuning
AnoGAN.best_for: Complex data distributions where GAN-based generation quality is high
AnoGAN.avoid_when: Fast inference is needed or training instability is a concern

strengths: Bi-directional GAN avoids slow iterative inference of AnoGAN
weaknesses: Complex architecture with multiple networks
Still subject to GAN training challenges
Many hyperparameters to tune
ALAD.best_for: Scenarios where GAN-based detection is desired but fast inference is needed
ALAD.avoid_when: Simpler reconstruction-based deep methods suffice or dataset is small

strengths: Jointly optimizes autoencoder and one-class SVM
Combines representation learning with boundary detection
weaknesses: Complex joint optimization
Requires tuning of both AE and SVM hyperparameters
Training can be unstable
AE1SVM.best_for: Datasets benefiting from joint representation learning and one-class classification
AE1SVM.avoid_when: Simpler pipeline of separate AE + SVM works well, or dataset is small

DevNet.full_name: Deep Anomaly Detection with Deviation Networks
strengths: Can leverage a few labeled anomalies (semi-supervised)
Deviation loss directly optimizes anomaly scores
weaknesses: Requires at least a few labeled anomalies for best results
Deep network training overhead
Sensitive to architecture and loss parameters
DevNet.best_for: Semi-supervised anomaly detection where a small number of labeled anomalies are available
DevNet.avoid_when: No labeled anomalies are available or dataset is too small for deep learning

strengths: Random walk on graph reveals connectivity anomalies
Tunable transition steps for multi-scale detection
weaknesses: Quadratic memory for adjacency/transition matrix
Slow for large datasets
Requires tuning of graph construction parameters
RGraph.best_for: Datasets where graph connectivity and neighborhood structure reveal anomalies
RGraph.avoid_when: Dataset is very large or a simpler proximity method suffices

complexity.time: O(n * k * d + n * h * epochs)

strengths: Learns to score anomalies from neighbor graphs via GNN
Combines neighborhood structure with learned representations
Supports both score and weight model types
weaknesses: Requires PyTorch for GNN training
More complex setup than classical methods
Training overhead from neural network
LUNAR.best_for: Datasets where learned neighborhood-based scoring outperforms handcrafted rules
LUNAR.avoid_when: PyTorch is not available or simpler KNN/LOF methods work well

strengths: Leverages foundation model embeddings for anomaly detection
Supports text and image data natively
weaknesses: Requires a pre-trained encoder model
Embedding quality depends on the foundation model
Higher memory usage for large embedding dimensions
EmbeddingOD.best_for: Anomaly detection on unstructured data (text, images) via foundation model representations
EmbeddingOD.avoid_when: Data is already tabular or a suitable encoder is not available

strengths: Combines multiple data modalities for anomaly detection
Leverages foundation model embeddings per modality
weaknesses: Requires encoder for each modality
Higher computational cost with multiple modalities
Combination strategy choice affects performance
MultiModalOD.best_for: Anomaly detection on multi-modal data combining text, image, or other modalities
MultiModalOD.avoid_when: Only a single modality is available or data is purely tabular

strengths: Zero-shot anomaly detection via LLM reasoning
Handles diverse data types through natural language understanding
weaknesses: Not yet implemented
Requires LLM API access with associated costs
Inference latency from LLM calls
LLMAD.best_for: Zero-shot or few-shot anomaly detection leveraging LLM world knowledge
LLMAD.avoid_when: Feature is needed before release or LLM API costs are prohibitive

strengths: Unified interface for time-series anomaly detection
weaknesses: Not yet implemented
Scope and supported methods to be determined
TimeSeriesOD.avoid_when: Data is not temporal or feature is needed before release
